sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for this shmup

special_mechanics.name: Aircraft Movement
special_mechanics.description: Player controls TwinBee (blue) or WinBee (pink) aircraft that maneuver through seven stages. Movement physics are typical for scrolling shooter genre with continuous directional control.

special_mechanics.name: Companion Ship Formation
special_mechanics.description: Three selectable companion ship behaviors: follow, engulf, or surround. Affects hitbox and movement interaction.

special_mechanics.name: Life Meter System
special_mechanics.description: Instead of discrete lives, ships have a damage-based life meter. No instantaneous death; ships can absorb multiple hits.

special_mechanics.name: Close-Range Melee
special_mechanics.description: Ships can attack airborne enemies with close-range punches in addition to ranged gun attacks.

special_mechanics.name: Ship Projectile Attack
special_mechanics.description: Player can shoot tiny ship replicas that attack on-screen enemies. Limited by stock count.

tier_notes: Tier 2: Empirical/qualitative data only. This is a scrolling shooter (shmup), not a platformer. Movement physics documentation is not available in public sources. Available sources contain gameplay mechanics description but no quantified physics parameters. Wikipedia provides game overview but lacks technical movement data.
